# Aleksandr Shulgin (IShu) — Code for any task

> Machine-readable mirror of https://shulgin.is-a.dev (the human version is a React SPA, RU/EN).
> Full-cycle engineer: backend Python, LLM pipelines, automation, Web3/trading infrastructure.
> I take a task in words and hand back a system that runs without me. One person, the whole path: code, infrastructure, 24/7 production.
> Open to offers: freelance or full-time, remote, GMT+10.

## Numbers

- **24/7** — unattended production uptime across the fleet
- **10+** — production systems live right now
- **x13** — latency gain vs baseline on the trading hot path
- **3.9M** — trades analyzed in edge research
- **2** — Erdos problems with Lean 4 proofs merged into Google DeepMind's formal-conjectures

## Flagship: Polymarket Market-Making System

One integrated trading system, results only (implementation details intentionally omitted — the repos are private):

- **Execution layer** — a series of trading bots: async Python orchestration with Rust on the hot path. Sub-50ms p99 order submission, a 13x latency gain. [Python, Rust, async]
- **Market-making engine** — liquidity quoting for a rewards program, careful position reconciliation, its own treasury. Around the clock, unattended. [CLOB, trading, treasury]
- **Edge research** — finding profitable traders: 3.9M trades, 6249 wallets, ranked by durable edge, manipulators filtered out. [analytics, EVM, data]
- **Low-latency backbone** — a market WebSocket multiplexer: one upstream connection feeding every bot, reconnect without data loss. [async, WebSockets, infra]
- **Strategy calibration** — genetic parameter optimization with walk-forward validation. +59% PnL versus manual tuning. [GA, backtest, NumPy]

## Formal mathematics

Machine-verified results produced by AI pipelines under my supervision. 0 `sorry` in the Lean kernel, clean axioms.

- **Erdos 1084** — f1(n) = n-1: the maximum number of unit-distance pairs among n points on a line. Merged: https://github.com/google-deepmind/formal-conjectures/pull/4245
- **Erdos 1052** — the 24-digit unitary perfect number, proved via multiplicativity of sigma-star. Merged: https://github.com/google-deepmind/formal-conjectures/pull/4244
- **Erdos 36 (minimum overlap)** — M(1)..M(5) proved by machine enumeration of balanced partitions.
- **OpenEvolve search** — an evolutionary LLM pipeline on the Erdos minimum-overlap problem converged in ~10 iterations to the SOTA numerical bound (99.5% match), same result on two independent backends, one-command verifier. Repo: https://github.com/Sanexxxx777/erdos-openevolve

## Selected projects

- **Content Factory** (web+LLM, live) — a content-generation platform for several channels. FastAPI backend, Vite frontend, multi-provider LLM orchestration with fallback, a worker queue, scheduled publishing. Running 24/7.
- **Crime Token Scan** (public, live) — a Telegram bot for deep crypto-asset analytics. A custom pipeline that collects and scores signals, an AI comment on every event. Running 24/7.
- **25.VPN** (commercial, live) — a VPN service on custom infrastructure. A Telegram bot for sign-up and renewals, multi-server rollout, protection against sharing and leaks. Paying users, 24/7.
- **Transaction Guardian** (client, live) — on-chain transaction monitoring: event subscriptions, filters by address and signature, Telegram alerts, a stats dashboard. Runs 24/7 for several clients.
- **Hub Research** (client, live) — an AI assistant for crypto research: gathers data from many sources, distills it into structured LLM reports, delivers through a bot.
- **Twitter Intel** (client, live) — X/Twitter monitoring across crypto projects with AI summaries, importance scoring and noise filtering. Telegram alerts with inline controls.
- **Sector Map** — a crypto-market dashboard with interactive heatmaps by sector. Python backend aggregating CEX/DEX data, vanilla JS + D3 frontend. Repo: https://github.com/Sanexxxx777/crypto-dashboard
- **Setup Manager** (live) — a web tool for managing environment config and deployment: presets, settings sync, fast bootstrap on a new machine.
- **NFA Dashboard** — an infrastructure-monitoring panel: server and service status, metrics, alerts, a daily Telegram digest. Demo: https://github.com/Sanexxxx777/nfa-dashboard-demo
- **Manga Translator** — a pipeline that downloads manga, OCR via an MIT detector, translation through the Gemini API, renders text back into bubbles, packs CBZ. Fully scheduled.
- **Job Radar** — a 17-module vacancy-aggregation pipeline: 15+ sources into one feed, LLM relevance scoring, dedup and normalization, worker orchestration, Telegram control.

## Desktop & apps

- **Spread Monitor** (macOS) — a Tauri desktop app tracking price spread across trading venues in real time. Repo: https://github.com/Sanexxxx777/spread-monitor
- **EDICT** (web game, in dev) — a browser god-sim: deterministic TypeScript + Vite engine, world progression and events, no heavy engines.
- **pm2-cockpit** (OSS, MIT) — an emergency process-control cockpit over Telegram: a pinned message with stop/start toggles for pm2 processes, confirmations, an allowlist. Repo: https://github.com/Sanexxxx777/pm2-cockpit
- **Desktop Utilities** (macOS) — a native Swift keyboard-layout switcher and Tauri task planners.

## Stack

- **Backend / Python**: Python 3.11+ (core), asyncio/aiohttp, FastAPI, pydantic, pytest
- **Automation / scraping**: Playwright (incl. anti-bot), Telethon, BeautifulSoup/lxml
- **LLM / AI**: multi-provider pipelines (Anthropic/OpenAI/Google/DeepSeek/Groq), agentic workflows, prompt engineering, Lean 4 formal verification pipelines
- **Web3 / trading**: CLOB market-making, on-chain monitoring (EVM), WebSocket infrastructure, backtesting
- **Web**: TypeScript, React, Vite, Node.js; Rust on hot paths; Tauri for desktop
- **Ops**: Linux servers, pm2, nginx, GitHub Actions, monitoring/alerting via Telegram

## How I work

- Results over process: every system above runs in production, most unattended.
- Honest metrics: numbers are measured, not estimated; private trading internals stay private — results are shown, methods are not.
- One person, full cycle: requirements → code → infrastructure → deploy → monitoring.
